text: Kashish, the way you hum when you're eating something delicious always makes me smile.
Our first WhatsApp chat that lasted until 4 AM. I was so sleepy at work, but it was worth it.
The day you wore my oversized hoodie and drowned in it. You looked so precious.
When you got cream on your nose during our first coffee date and glared when I laughed.
Dancing under that tiny umbrella in the summer downpour. Drenched but completely happy.
The way your eyes wrinkle when you laugh at my terrible dad jokes.
Falling asleep on my shoulder ten minutes into the train ride. I held my breath so you wouldn't wake.
Our endless debates about whether pizza is better than burgers. You always win anyway.
Kashish, your hand fits so perfectly in mine, like it was shaped just for it.
When you stayed up late just to keep me company while I finished my work.
The silly hand-painted mug you gave me. I still drink my tea from it every morning.
Watching the sparklers reflect in your eyes on our anniversary night.
Our spontaneous road trip sing-alongs. Your voice is my favorite radio station.
How you play with your hair when you're deep in thought. It's beautiful.
Waking up to your 'good morning' text is the best part of my day, Kashish.
When we got lost in the coastal highway and ended up watching the sunset from the hood of the car.
The warm hugs you give me when I'm stressed. They instantly melt my worries away.
How you always steal the blankets in your sleep. I don't mind the cold as long as you're warm.
Your beautiful, soft smile that makes the entire world fade into the background.
That cozy winter cabin in the hills, drinking cocoa and writing down our future house plans.
Kashish, you make every ordinary day feel like a luxury holiday.
The messy cooking sessions where we ended up ordering take-out because we burnt the pasta.
How you hold my arm when crossing the street. It makes me feel so protective.
The sweet voice notes you send me just to say you miss me. I loop them constantly.
Wiping a stray tear from your cheek when we watched that emotional movie.
Your absolute obsession with cute stationery and notebooks that you never write in.
The way you say my name with that soft, loving tone that sends shivers down my spine.
Our quiet walks by the lake promenade under the pink cherry blossoms.
When we sat in silence on the pier for hours, just listening to the waves, knowing we were home.
How you always know exactly what I'm thinking just by looking at my face.
Your kind, generous heart that always wants to help every stray puppy we pass.
Sharing a single waffle and fighting over the last strawberry slice.
Kashish, you are my quiet haven in this loud, busy world.
The late-night kitchen raids where we ate cereal in our pyjamas at 2 AM.
How you glare at me when I tease you, but your lips twitch trying not to smile.
The scent of your perfume that lingers on my jacket long after you've gone home.
When you held my hand tighter during the bumpy flight. I felt so safe beside you.
Watching you sleep peacefully beside me, feeling incredibly lucky to call you mine.
The goofy faces you make in my contact photo on your phone.
Your determination to learn driving, even when you accidentally hit the trash can.
Kashish, you are the most beautiful chapter in my diary of life.
How you remember the smallest details about me that even I forget.
Our first photo together, squeezed tight, cheeks flushed, and smiling wide.
The way you curl your toes when you are cold. So adorable.
When you spent hours picking the perfect tie for my interview. I got the job because of you.
Drinking hot tea in the balcony during the first monsoon breeze.
Your cute habit of buying plants and naming them after our future children.
Kashish, loving you is as natural as breathing, and just as vital.
The way you light up whenever you see chocolate or pastries.
The promise we made to hold hands and walk together even when we are eighty and wrinkled.
Our ridiculous nicknames for each other that we only use when we're alone.
How you always put my comfort before yours. You have the kindest soul.
The warmth of your forehead pressed against mine on a quiet evening.
When you tried to teach me yoga and we ended up rolling on the floor laughing.
The little doodles you draw on the margins of my notebooks when I'm not looking.
Kashish, you are my favorite distraction and my greatest inspiration.
Your clumsy walks that always lead to you tripping over flat carpets.
The soft humming sound you make when you're sleeping. It's my lullaby.
How you make even a trip to the grocery store feel like an exciting date.
The proud look on your face when you finally baked a cake that didn't sink in the middle.
The feeling of peace that washes over me whenever you rest your head on my chest.
How you always take a bite of my food even after claiming you're not hungry.
Kashish, you are the gravity that keeps my chaotic world from spinning apart.
Our spontaneous afternoon movie tickets on rainy Tuesdays.
The way you scold me for not eating on time, showing how much you care.
When you wore that saree for the festival and left me completely speechless.
The warm breeze blowing your hair across your face as we stood on the deck.
How you always find my lost keys when I've searched the entire house thrice.
Kashish, every corner of my heart is filled with thoughts of you.
The quiet nights sitting on the roof, looking at the stars, talking about life.
The cute way you pout when I steal your favorite fries.
Your laugh that rings like little silver bells, driving away all my sadness.
How you squeeze my hand three times to secretly say 'I love you' in public.
Your loyalty, strength, and unwavering belief in me when I doubted myself.
The beautiful card you wrote for my last birthday. I keep it in my wallet always.
Kashish, you are the best decision I have ever made in my life.
The funny faces we make at each other across the room at boring parties.
How you look so pretty in the morning sunlight, without a trace of makeup.
Sharing a warm blanket and watching our favorite sitcoms on repeat.
The way you tuck your chin when you feel shy or embarrassed.
Kashish, my love for you grows deeper with every rising sun.
How you always make sure my collar is straight before I walk out the door.
The spontaneous walks to get ice cream in the middle of cold winter nights.
Your gorgeous handwriting that looks like art on paper.
The way you hold my face in your hands when you look into my eyes.
Kashish, you have taught me the true meaning of selfless, unconditional love.
Our cozy afternoon naps where we end up sleeping for four hours instead of thirty minutes.
How you get excited like a kid when it starts to rain.
The sweet promise of holding your hand through every high and low of this life.
Your little habits of talking to yourself when you're focusing on a task.
Kashish, you are my today, my tomorrow, and my forever absolute.
The way you protectively stand up for me whenever someone tries to tease me.
Sharing the driver's seat and navigating traffic jams with silly car games.
Your gentle, soothing voice that instantly calms my racing thoughts.
The warm memory of our first hug, feeling the exact click of our hearts matching.
How you keep every rose I buy you, pressing them inside thick books.
Kashish, you have made my life a beautiful, glowing fairytale.
The quiet breakfasts we share, eating burnt toast but smiling anyway.
Thank you for choosing me, for loving me, and for being my absolute home.
Happy Birthday, my love. I cannot wait to create a million more memories with you, Kashish.
